library_preparation_preflight.summary: The artifact is a self-contained Angus Mail core/utilities jar with transitive Jakarta Mail and Activation dependencies; no extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed, but one JDK module export may be required for full SocketFetcher SSL hostname-check coverage.
library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Meaningful tests can use in-memory streams, Jakarta Activation DataHandler/DataSource objects, ServiceLoader for jakarta.mail.util.StreamProvider, and loopback ServerSocket/SSLServerSocket instances; do not require live SMTP/IMAP/POP3 infrastructure. If exercising com.sun.mail.util.SocketFetcher server identity verification through the JDK sun.security.util.HostnameChecker path, configure JVM/native test execution with --add-exports=java.base/sun.security.util=ALL-UNNAMED, as the related Angus Mail tests do.
risks: Most public packages in angus-core are utility/internal support packages, so generated tests should avoid adding angus-mail or protocol artifacts just to test SMTP/IMAP/POP3 behavior.
SocketFetcher has fallback hostname matching when sun.security.util is inaccessible, so lack of the add-exports option may reduce coverage of that specific path rather than block all useful coverage.
